food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken burger with large fries and large soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a double-patty spicy fried chicken thigh burger meal combo. FOODON_00002383 (chicken sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class. The upsized meal combo (large fries + large drink) has no direct FoodOn representation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKR (chicken burger, fast food) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for a double-patty spicy chicken thigh burger upsized meal combo in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a branded fast food combo meal at this specificity. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double McSpicy burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a Double McSpicy burger (two whole-muscle spicy fried chicken thigh patties in a sesame bun with lettuce and mayonnaise), one large portion of french fries, and one large soft drink, totalling approximately 900 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McSpicy and Fries/Beverage components
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (via USDA FoodData Central analogous fast-food entries for per-100g normalisation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-serving nutrition for individual menu items. The Double McSpicy burger (two spicy fried chicken thigh patties, sesame bun, lettuce, mayonnaise) was estimated by doubling the single McSpicy patty contribution and adding bun/condiment values consistent with McDonald's Singapore published data. Large fries and large cola values were drawn from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for fast-food french fries (USDA FDC ID 170720) and cola beverage (USDA FDC ID 174851). The composite meal was assembled and normalised to per-100g basis using the estimated total meal weight of 900 g. All per-100g values represent the blended composite of the full upsized meal.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white wheat bread and fast-food french fries analogues; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Double McSpicy Upsized Meal as a composite. GI was estimated as a weighted composite: the sesame wheat bun component uses GI ~72 (white wheat bread analogue, University of Sydney GI Database); the large french fries component uses GI ~63 (fast-food french fries, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021); the cola component uses GI ~63 (cola beverage, University of Sydney GI Database); the fried chicken thigh patties contribute minimal glycaemic load (GI ~0 for protein/fat-dominant component). Weighted composite GI estimated at 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 211.5) / 100 ≈ 137, rounded to 136.0 g carbohydrate per 900 g meal (23.5 g per 100 g × 9 = 211.5 g total carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — approximately 2,790 mg sodium per full upsized meal serving (310 mg per 100 g × 900 g), exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 140% of the WHO recommended daily sodium intake; if consumed, portion reduction and avoidance of added condiments are strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 136) driven by the large refined-carbohydrate load from the sesame bun, large french fries, and cola beverage, combined with high total energy density.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal due to its very high glycemic load (GL ≈ 136 per serving), which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed occasionally, substituting the cola with water and reducing fries portion size are the minimum recommended modifications.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium content in a single ultra-processed fast-food meal, with total energy approximately 1,782 kcal per serving.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal, as its combination of high saturated fat (~18.9 g per serving), trans fat (~0.9 g per serving), sodium (~2,790 mg per serving), and ultra-processed ingredients represents a significant cumulative cardiovascular risk burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double McSpicy Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast-food combo meal consisting of a Double McSpicy burger — two whole-muscle spicy fried chicken thigh patties encased in a crispy seasoned batter, served in a toasted sesame-seed bun with shredded lettuce and mayonnaise — accompanied by a large portion of salted french fries and a large cola soft drink. The meal is exclusive to McDonald's Singapore and is a larger, double-patty variant of the iconic McSpicy burger, which has been a flagship Singapore menu item since the 1990s. The chicken thighs are marinated in a proprietary spice blend and deep-fried to produce a crunchy, fiery exterior with a juicy interior. The meal is certified Halal by MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura). Nutritionally, the full upsized meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 1,782 kcal per serving, with a high carbohydrate load (~211 g, predominantly from refined sources), moderate-to-high protein (~70 g from the double chicken thigh patties), and significant fat content (~74 g, including saturated and trans fats from deep-frying). Sodium content is very high at approximately 2,790 mg per serving, substantially exceeding the WHO daily recommended limit. The glycemic load is very high (GL ≈ 136), making this meal clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. This meal is best regarded as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary component, particularly for individuals with cardiometabolic risk factors.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-serving nutrition data for individual menu items on its official website, which is an approved secondary reference for branded commercial products. However, the Double McSpicy Upsized Meal as a composite (double burger + large fries + large cola) required assembly and per-100g normalisation from component values, with cross-referencing against USDA FoodData Central fast-food analogues. No single approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA FDC) contains a direct entry for this exact composite meal. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100g values represent the blended nutritional composite of the full upsized meal (estimated total weight 900 g: Double McSpicy burger ~350 g + large fries ~170 g + large cola ~380 g including ice). 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data nor in the USDA FDC fast-food analogues used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data for this product. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data nor in the USDA FDC analogues used. 5. GI is an analogous estimate (weighted composite GI ≈ 65, medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white wheat bread (bun component), fast-food french fries, and cola beverage; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. 6. The meal is Halal-certified by MUIS Singapore. 7. Trans fat content (~0.1 g per 100 g) arises from deep-frying; total trans fat per serving is approximately 0.9 g. 8. The food_id GD000106 is a field survey data seed record; this record is not present in the FD-series food_index.json. 9. Serving size of 900 g is an estimate based on typical McDonald's Singapore upsized meal component weights; actual weights may vary slightly by outlet.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Assembled composite meal nutrient values from McDonald's Singapore published data and USDA FDC fast-food analogues; normalised to per-100g basis for 900g estimated meal weight.

Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list; confirmed all Tamil, Hindi, Chinese, and Malay entries appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) are documented in notes. Verified GI is numeric >0 with gi_category set.
